title: Phaenopyrum carolinianum
intro: Phaenopyrum carolinianum, also known as the Carolina buckthorn or buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. Phaenopyrum carolinianum contains a variety of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs, to support digestive health and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Phaenopyrum carolinianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Nasturcum, but it is sometimes classified under the family Tropaeolaceae. It is an annual herb native to eastern and central North America. The plant belongs to the order Brassicales and the class Magnoliopsida. Its classification is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae. This plant is also sometimes referred to as 'Carolina goldenraintree' due to its distinctive golden-colored flowers.
